Lucas Dewey
Experience Contact
Back to experience

2019-2022

Lawrence Livermore National Lab

Mechanical Engineer - Product Realization

Designed, analyzed, tested, and released stainless steel and polymer components for DOE program hardware at LLNL, supporting forged, machined, additively manufactured, and molded part development from design trade studies through first-article production, inspection, and qualification.

Location Livermore, CA

Highlights

  • Supported DOE program hardware across stainless steel AM, forged, machined, HDPE, and silicone foam components.
  • Owned design analysis, trade studies, GD&T drawings, test planning, material characterization, and manufacturing qualification work.
  • Collaborated directly with manufacturing teams through first-article builds to resolve production, post-processing, and inspection issues.
  • Helped qualify critical components for use while improving manufacturability, assembly alignment, and production readiness.

Tools

SolidWorksGD&TDFM/DFAEngineering drawingsAdditive manufacturingLaser powder bed fusionForgingMachining316L stainless steelMaterial characterizationAccelerated life cycle testingFirst-principles calculationsTrade studiesFirst-article productionInspection supportSilicone foam processingLiquid silicone rubber

Public recognition

The W80-4 Porcula Team received public recognition in LLNL's Defense Programs Awards of Excellence coverage for rapid design iteration and manufacturability improvements supporting the W80-4 Life Extension Program.

"During a time of tremendous scrutiny and scheduling pressure, the LLNL Porcula team overcame numerous obstacles including unforeseen technical delays. The team developed and implemented a baseline (Catamaran) and backup (Sunfish) design of a key component for the W80-4 Life Extension Program, which significantly increased achievability of its First Production Unit."

Source: LLNL website

Projects

Work at Lawrence Livermore National Lab

Project

Defence program design and manufacturing

Helped resolve manufacturing and design challenges for high-consequence hardware by supporting rapid CAD updates, drawing release, inspection planning, and production troubleshooting.

Contribution

  • Created CAD updates and part drawings to support fast design-build-test cycles
  • Worked with manufacturing and inspection teams to address production, post-processing, and verification issues
  • Assisted with metallographic inspection work to validate design intent and manufacturing quality

Result

Supported the Product Realization Team, which received NNSA recognition for rapidly developing viable design options, improving manufacturability, and increasing first-production readiness.
